review_title: Professional powerhouse
description: I recommend this software to anyone who'll listen. I typically have a love/hate relationship with productivity software, but this is a love/love relationship. I'm confident of its capabilities and acts as an extension of superior client service. Hats off to the BugHerd team!!
author: Iole T.
pros: I've never worked with more convenient software....it actually saves time, which is often-promised and never a reality. While it's convenient for production, the key feature that is most beneficial is allowing non-technical users (think account leaders, PMs, but particularly clients) to simply point and click to identify an issue. It takes a screenshot of where the issue was identified, so even if detail is missing from the logged issue, the software provides the solution.
cons: I love it too much to have a least-liked feature, but if I had to point to something, I'd like the ability to have an 'explainer' within the projects, so if someone goes to a project, they know which URLs to review, expectations, timelines, etc., versus having to capture that information separately. At times it's been buggy, but nothing that was a deal-breaker; for example, it'll move tasks back to their original status, even if marked completed. I only experienced this 2x in 2 years.

review_title: This tool pays for inself very quickly
description: Over the 30+ years that I have been working in the education industry, I have never encountered a more effective or efficient tool. Bugherd does exactly what it was designed to do, and it does so very well for a reasonable price. I cannot say enough good about this product and the support provided by this company.
author: Kimberly G.
pros: Clear user interface that anyone can quickly learn to use. Flexibility so that it can be adapted to the needs of each project.
cons: There isn't anything that I dislike about it.

review_title: Great for client and QA collaboration
author: Dealdiane D.
pros: This is a great tool for when you want clients and quality assurance testers to be able to mark items that needs attention.
cons: Needs more (built-in) integration with major bug tracking softwares. Marker can sometimes point to a wrong location but screenshots can also be taken when creating an issue as a solution to that

review_title: Get BugHerd and never get another bug list over email.
description: Love it! We use it every day.
author: Andrew M.
pros: Clients and team love using this tool. It’s easy to figure out and use for everyone.
cons: No way to see items assigned to you across all projects.

review_title: Our clients LOVE it
author: Chris S.
pros: To us, the client experience reigns supreme. Also, we hated getting two mile long emails with website edits. With BugHerd, our website review process and ongoing support has become incredibly efficient. Our clients love knowing they can add a note to their website to get our eyes on it, and we MUCH prefer having a list of easy to understand BH notes to deal with instead of trying to interpret email chains!
cons: BugHerd keep improving - so I have nothing negative to say. All the main features I could ask for already exist, and they have made major improvements to their interface and client side implementation process.

review_title: Very handy tool in web development
description: vera satisfied and very excited with new features implemented in this handy tool
author: Štefan S.
pros: Very handy and usefull software that helps organize various tasks during developing a new website. Aditional information collected into specific task are very helpfull for developers, and also captured screenshot and possilibility to add files to task (for example video)
cons: sometimes its problem to capture screenshots of website.

review_title: A no-brainer purchase for any agency or development team
description: A pleasure to use. Easy to install and use and it makes QA testing a delight.
author: Mark B.
pros: It removes any ambiguity about bugs and feature requests. Being able to annotate a comment directly on a live / staging website, is a huge perk and something that I now cannot live without.
cons: The lack of customizable columns in their kanban-style layout is a pity. Not a deal breaker, but our agency workflow does not follow a 'backlog', 'doing', 'done', 'closed' workflow and we end up having to have internal discussions about what 'backlog' actually represents in our own workflow. Would be great to rename these columns and have more/less of them.

review_title: Efficient and easy to use QA tool
description: We've been using Bugherd for several years for website QA tasks and the experience is great so far. We have not thought of changing this tool, it serves its purpose 100%. Easy to use, the drawing and the tagging really help with identifying the exact location of bugs.
author: Stella D.
pros: the drawing and the tagging really help with identifying the exact location of bugs
cons: Nothing so far, even when our team had contacted the support in the past to sort out the lack of some features, the Bugherd dev team managed to update their product and those features were finally added

review_title: I love bugherd!
author: Elizabeth M.
pros: very easy to use, easy to report issues and sort them. usefull to have context for the issues.
cons: I have nothing to report about the cons.

review_title: Best tool for web design
description: I have been using BugHerd for many years and it is simply the best. All of my clients love it and it has simplified the website review process so much. I don't know what I would do without this tool. It is amazing.
author: Lee G.
pros: Easy of use and ability to tag the specific spot that needs correcting on websites.
cons: Sometimes the email notifications don't notify you of new bugs on the site.

review_title: Wonderful product that does exactly what is says it does.
description: Wonderful product, at a great price does exactly what is says it does. Loved the easy to use Kanban. One of the best products we have used.
author: Chuck S.
pros: Simple to deploy, simple to use. Even the technically unskilled were able to note issues that needed attention. We used BugHerd to successfully maintain a 600 plus page website through two CMS changes, and it worked flawlessly.
cons: A bit difficult to note changes on mobile, but it seems like that has been addressed now.

review_title: Bug Tracking Done Right
description: This is a product that has proven its value over the years. The time savings and overall efficiencies for website projects (managing front end builds and CMS integration) are amazing since we started using BugHerd.
author: Scott J.
pros: The Intuitive and robust nature of the product makes it an invaluable tool that we can not live without.
cons: The Mobile Support could be better BUT it is in the works.

review_title: Makes our Team more Efficient
description: Using Bugherd has allowed me to keep a consistent workflow, even when work needs to be QA'd. Using the tool makes the process quick and efficient, and I'm able to communicate thoroughly with the engineers on my team.
author: Alicia C.
pros: What I love most about this software is the ease of use. I can translate bugs to the developers on my team so easily, and quickly! We are a fast paced company and this helps keep it that way.
cons: Ideally, I would love a mobile version - so I can test on my device (rather than just the in browser inspect mode for mobile). Other than that, I love the product.

review_title: Solid solution for building SaaS products and websites
author: ANDREW P.
pros: Easy to use. Intuitive. Makes communicating with developers and reporting bugs, issues, and requests about as easy as can be
cons: I really wish there was a 2-way flow with Jira.

review_title: Must have tool for project launch
description: With bugherd we have a tool to assign tasks to frontend, backend, designer and project owner.
author: Nico H.
pros: It is easy to make the client to understand the work flow. With this tool we accelerate the project launch.
cons: It could be useful to have the option to close the feedback round.

review_title: BugHerd
description: Very positive: great product; great team.
author: Ted B.
pros: The ability to tie a bug directly to a course page; the browser extension; automatic screenshots; discussions; the new ability add/rename swimlanes.
cons: We'd like to be able to group individual courses in Canvas (each with a unique URL) into the same BH project: currently unable to do so. The annotation feature for screenshots is a bit primitive. Want to be able to edit link URLs in case page names (and therefore URLs) change after a bug is logged.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Track your bugs and tasks easily
author: Dennis H.
pros: - Easy to use for customers - Connection between ticket and webpage frontend clarifies the task at hand and minimizes feedback loops - UI is very cleaned up and intuitive
cons: - No Jira integration - I have to write at least 40 characters in this box... ;-)

review_title: Take the guesswork out of website edits
description: We use it for QA on all of our websites, both for our internal teams and clients.
author: Nick S.
pros: Internal and external stakeholders can mark directly on the page of a website any edits or bugs. No more taking screen shots and marking them up, or trying to read hand-written notes, or even worse decipher email feedback. All tasks are marked in the backend, in a task board.
cons: Doesn't work very well on mobile. Also could use some approval levels within the software.

review_title: Useful? YES!
description: This helps us a lot to fix web issues and responsive problems that are key points on our work.
author: Reyniel C.
pros: The easier way to manage issues is a big thing that I love.
cons: I think I don't have anything to say on this, I'm very happy with the product.

review_title: The best review software for clients
author: Justin H.
pros: I searched through every website review product I could think of and BugHerd came out on top. The ease of use for clients and non-technical users sold us. It also works seamlessly with or without the browser plug-in.
cons: The only negative about BugHerd is that I would prefer if the seats were per project and not on the overall account. We have different account people for different projects and I can't add who I need for each project so I find myself adding and removing people regularly.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: website deployment
description: It was easy to use, simply hovering over subject matter allowed me to drill in exactly where I wanted to address issues for my developer located on the other side of the world.
author: Wes E.
pros: Targeted communication of website changes needed to be made, allowed for screen shots to provide more clarity.
cons: I have no negative feedback on the BugHerd product.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: BugHerd Review
author: Shivani N.
pros: BugHerd makes it super easy to submit QA tickets and see them in one place. In addition, it's super easy to collaborate with others on these tickets and take actions. I really enjoy the snapshot feature and ability to put a ticket on a mockup itself.
cons: BugHerd could be a little mroe visually engaging and interesting in its design approach.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Good tool for web agency
description: Very good experience, the team is nice, the support efficient
author: Axel S.
pros: Very good tools ! Very usefull for my web agency
cons: in mobile IOS, the user has to make some modification which is not simple every time
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: An essential tool for serious project management
description: This product is exceptional, without it web and software development would be much harder to manage, I manage multiple projects each having multiple members, the thought of tracking bugs from multiple sources like I used to in email and spreadsheets would literally take for ever to track, update and share, BugHerd has saved me and my team thousands of wasted hours!
author: Elliott R.
pros: Projected based in-browser centralised tasks in one place makes project management a breeze, also having comments in one thread related to a single task helps to pinpoint and focused on bug tracking that others products just can’t do well. I couldn’t bear to go back to email or spreadsheet task list!
cons: As projects grow and users need to be added to different projects the cost can get expensive, be aware that the lift from 10 users to 25,50 isn’t obvious from the outset, know this in advance so it can be absorbed in your SLA’s

review_title: Fast bug tracking for bulk bugs, with slow development of new features
description: I've been using BugHerd for around 3 years now on large scale web projects, often with 500-2000 bugs reported throughout the project life. I've recently tested all of the key bug tracking tools available for website development, and BugHerd is hands down, still the fastest and most useful tool for bug reporting with a useful user area for bug management. The tool could be so much better though if the dev team behind it invested some more time and resources to make bulk bug management and integrations better.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Fast bug reporting with easy to set tags, users and descriptions. Easy to filter user area with kanban columns, search and the ability to go click on a bug and be anchored to a pin in the bugs location on your website. No need to install code for each website you test.
cons: The development team is very slow to update and make changes to BugHerd. BugHerd is lacking bulk select features, and their connections to third party tools with better backend management aren't up to scratch.
